description: Grace, beauty, elegance - these terms embody orchids like no other plant. The magnificent exotics were cultivated already 2,500 years ago in the Chinese Empire and were considered the queen among the flowers. The Aztecs also planted orchids in the gardens of their sanctuaries. Orchids are distributed worldwide. Its 1000 genera with up to 30,000 species produce an incredible variety of shapes and colors. Most orchids grow on trees in their homeland to get more light in the rainforest. With the roots, the beautiful exotics absorb some of the necessary nutrients from the air and rainwater. For this reason, orchids may never soil, but need special substrate. If you pour them too much, orchids will quickly get offended and, in the worst case, get in.
